title: calocedrus decurrens
intro: Calocedrus decurrens, also known as the incense cedar, is a coniferous tree native to the western United States and Mexico, characterized by its distinctive, fragrant wood and needle-like leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Calocedrus decurrens include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and promote relaxation due to its calming scent. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, essential oils, and topical creams, allowing for a variety of uses in aromatherapy and natural remedies.
